content.thinking:

While some ants do have wings (like queens and males during mating season), ants are NOT commonly known as flying insects. Most ants people encounter are wingless workers, and ants are generally thought of as crawling insects that live on the ground or in colonies.

So the answer should be "no".

Most ant species don't feed on or damage wooden structures. Ants are more commonly known for other behaviors like scavenging for food, farming aphids, etc.

Carpenter ants do damage wood, but they don't eat it - they excavate it for nesting. And this is just one type of ant among thousands of species.

So the answer should be "no" because ants in general are not primarily known for feeding on wood or damaging wooden structures. That would be more characteristic of termites.
